lines.content: Hey, Tyler, this is Calvin at Einstein moving, how are you doing today?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: Doing well man, thanks for asking. Yeah, just wanted to reach out for this phone interview it'll be about ten minutes or so but yeah my name's Calvin, I I'm the branch manager here at our north Austin location. I, I've been with Einstein a little over ten years now actually I started out just doing a phone interview but yeah, all of our management positions are from within. We've got now nine Texas branches and one in Florida and yeah, we're just always trying to grow and expand our team and just checking in. So, you, you've been a caregiver for eight years and just kind of looking for a change or what are you looking for like extra work. Or what, what's the deal there?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: So essentially I've been in retail since I was fifteen years old and when I was trying to go into the military, originally I unfortunately got denied due to a minor health concern that I couldn't change. And then my uncle or my grandfather and my Ana got sick as they got older grand unfortunately passed away and then just recently with nana, she didn't pass away but she was demented and had to basically be put into a care facility.
So, what I'm trying to do now is essentially brought in my horizon after now that I don't have to always be there to take care of her.
Then you were going to be looking to work full-time do you have, what's your, are you pretty open availability any days you can't work?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: Nothing really set that way. The only like the only appointment I have coming up is a doctor's appointment one and Tuesday but there's no like blackout days for my schedule obviously.
Okay. Yeah, yeah, you can always request days off too and and things like that, so no worries and you have a valid driver's license with two years driving history.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: And I guess at this time, what kind of maybe are you know, ultimately going to be some of your career goals or ambitions?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: Right now, I'm just trying to find a place where I can truly fit in and belong since, for the long time I've been in the retail environment trying to move up and forward. Like right now, I'm currently at Walgreens. I tried to move to the pharmacy itself but there's one reason or another they're not allowing me to move forward because of lack of available hours. And then, when I tried to apply for assistant manager is the case of just the store that I'm currently at is too small to accept that. So yeah.

And then obviously, you know, that's the moving job. It's, you know, the, a lot of the guys love the kind of physical nature of it but it is obviously A-F physically demanding job, you know, hot, hot days. And, you know, some, some days can be a little longer any, any concerns there?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: No, not really. I've been doing pretty well regarding being able to do that, I don't know if you would notice with my, with my application. I had said that I do have a disability my disability being that I actually am a tie to diabetic but mine doesn't require insulin. So I actually had lost £100 in the last nine months. So I like to think I'm good to handle it pretty well and I've been living in Texas all thirty years in my life.
Hold on. Is that, why is that, why the military didn't take us the diabetes?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: Well, I'm just not taking care of myself and everything. The reason they didn't take me, they supposedly said something about like, they put it as they compared it to a tip of a pencil racer on my table and saying, oh, we want to remove this because we're making hard for you to do setups, which made no sense because I was through J-R-T-C, through all my high-school life and I never had any trouble doing that and then they just cleaned. That was twenty pounds overweight.
Yeah, it was very very odd, I was gonna originally go to be a radar technician or administrative duties in the Navy.

Gotcha. Yeah I had a guy recently who yeah, he was trying to do the military and they rejected them cause it's like, he has like egg. It's like alright but as far as your professional strengths, what do you think are three of your professional strengths?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: I definitely say one of them is my attention to detail cause I like to view myself as someone who notices the little things. Those are always a big thing to me just to make sure that nothing has forgotten. I would say another one is the fact that I'm a quick learner like even if I don't have too much experience in one area I always feel I learned fast to make sure that I do the job right the first time. So there isn't an issue, I don't say I don't make mistakes but if I make one I try to make sure it never happens again.
And then the other would be just how I function in the teamwork environment. I'm really look forward to be a part of a team and I try and do my best to get to know the people there and.

Nice. Yeah that, yeah, I, that's kind of one of the reasons I've been here so long and moved up and it's just a lot of good people and yeah, guys, I've really gotten to know friends with and, and all of that. So obviously, it's not required for guys to be friends or anything like that. I mean, work together well but we do quarterly parties and different team building events and it's always a, a super good time as far as, so on the flip side of that Tyler and this one might be a little bit of a harder question, but what do you think give you a couple of your weaknesses?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: That one for sure. I would definitely say confidence for sure. Like when I start out at first I'm very quiet, like more of a observer than I am, someone who will try and make myself known right away. I'm trying to work on that. But it's just unfortunately how I've always been especially right now. Like having to be cut off for essentially and not have my friendship circles or anything and unfortunately having to lose that way of talking to people like for a longest time. My only way of interact from people with the customers that work or my coworkers.
Sure, you're right. This is a little bit difficult and asking you looking words normally I'm always good about that but.
Yeah, if you have one more or maybe even like you know, maybe like a past employer, but they would say could have improved on.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: With that one. It was definitely leadership at the time. Even though I had originally been in the leadership program for so long I had gotten used to just being in the background for the longest time. But now recently being in the position, I am as a shift lead, I feel that has been something I've definitely been growing on and improving on.
How about questions, what questions can I answer for you?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: You said you've been there ten years yourself. What did you start out as?

Mm-hmm I just started as a mover. Yeah, I was playing music and, and kind of my banded broken up and I was thinking about being a teacher just because I had my degree in English. So I was kinda looking to do that and was moving with Einstein and and there was an ops manager position that opened up at north Austin. So I applied for that and got that and then I think it was about seven years ago. They, yeah, they put me in-charge of the north Austin location.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: I remember reading this thing. It said that you have the moving hours and then the waiting hours. So I understand you get like the main Chuck you pay when you're actually moving things into the truck and everything and then when you're in the truck driving that's when it's a little bit of a different pay scale, right?

No, you're the everyone when they come in, they're leading training they all are paid $20 an hour all day and then when you move up to it'll be position you go up to $21 an hour. In the first lead tier and lead movers. Actually, they can Ed quarterly performance bonuses as well. And it ranges from like two to up to like $3 more per hour, depending on like your level of lead that you're at. And then you do make tips, tips are really good, it's usually about $7 an hour extra that you're making in tips.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: I guess I would also say what has been one of the more not including the fact of just moving the things themselves like has never been any real struggles when it comes to doing this job. Like, say maybe just a client when you're moving something and they have complaints or something like, maybe they don't want you moving something a certain way or they feel you're being too rough.

Yeah, I definitely, I've dealt with you know a lot of different you know different client complaints over the years you know, with I mean, I had one today where you know, she had a move yesterday and it took longer than the estimate and you know, she was a little disappointed by that and, and stuff, but those things are up just like, really used to it you know, I've dealt with that a lot so I'm pretty, usually pretty easy conversation. I'd say most of the time like you know, if you're doing a good job doing things the right way and you know, providing good customer service like the clients, with the movers are usually pretty, pretty solid, nice you know, we have good a good client base as well. You know, I would say probably a lot of the times where they would get frustrated is usually if it's like a big difference in what the move ended up costing compared to what was estimated.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: So we, we've gotten a lot better with our estimates over the years or just kept honing them in tracking it better like different questions and then, then just really get into the to the right details and we're more accurate with, with our estimates than ever, but it, at the end of the day and they are still estimates and you know things can get missed or clients can, you know not forget to tell us about something or they might not pack everything up well enough and that makes the move go long. But yeah, I think that's probably the main, the main issue when a client and get some kind of up to.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: Cause I mean, things are going to break too from time to time, you know. It's like, we don't expect guys to never have damages, you know. Even having $1 an hour in damages is actually really good performance. You know, just limiting like, the big ticket damages like obviously wood floors are really expensive. So we don't slide stuff on those or like breaking the TV. So we tried to avoid the big ones obviously, but we have a whole damage department built out that handles damages. So, if, you know something does break, we file a claim follow up quickly. We resolve it. We go well above and beyond like, you know, the insurance we cover things really well and, and can make everything right almost like, all the time something did does go wrong.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: Gotcha. I think my last question would be is it usually like say two people per job, at least.
Yeah, at least two but also we'll do you know three man moves for four man, two trucks, three trucks, so, lots of different size moves.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: Yeah, man. Well, next step we would just bring in for an in person interview. So looking at next week Monday is pretty stacked but I got Tuesday I could do like a one or a 2:00 PM Wednesday. I could do ten to even 3:00 PM Thursday is wide open. Did you have a preference on day, day and time?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: I would say the twenty fifth on Thursday. Is that possible?
Yeah. What time did you want to do?
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: How about around either noon or 1:00?

Oh, yeah, let's do one o'clock and I'm gonna send you over an email it's gonna let you know where we're at. There will be a link to a form in it as well that you'll need to fill out. It just ask for like some references a bio about yourself things like that and then outside of that, did you have any any other questions.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes

lines.content: No, I believe that'll be about it sir. Thank you.
Awesome man. Have a great weekend.
lines.name: Calvin Hughes
